config.cache_mechanism: --cache_dir (OVMS folds it into the CB-LLM node plugin_config as CACHE_DIR)
config.cache_dir_path_format: FORWARD-SLASH (C:/Users/mrbla/agentic-setup/state/ovms-model-cache). A Windows BACKSLASH path makes the folded plugin_config JSON invalid (\U is not a valid JSON escape) -> 'Plugin config is in wrong format' (the #740/W7 revert cause).
config.other_flags: --cache_size 4 (KV-cache POOL, separate concern), --kv_cache_precision u8, --enable_prefix_caching true, --enable_tool_guided_generation true

measurements.cold_load_note: first load with an empty cache: compile + write the .cl_cache (log: 10:16:19 servable init -> 10:21:08 state AVAILABLE). One-time; also recurs on cache invalidation (OVMS/driver/model change).
measurements.warm_load_note: cache hit: load the compiled kernels, no recompile (start-llm.ps1 wall-clock, exit 0, model served).
measurements.baseline_no_cache_s_range: 30-90 (the pre-cache load range documented in start-llm.ps1)

not_measured: inference throughput (tokens/s) — this measures MODEL LOAD time only, not generation
co-resident memory cost with the 14B (the swap unloads the 14B before loading the 30B)
a statistical distribution — n=1 per condition; load time is low-variance, not sampled
the exact no-cache baseline on THIS box (used the script's documented 30-90s range, not a fresh cache-disabled run)
methodology: start-llm.ps1 -Model coder-30b -Force, wall-clock timed. COLD = empty cache dir (first ever); WARM = the cache populated by the cold run. Load success = coder-30b appears in GET :8000/v3/models. Headless, no GUI.

consequence.deadline_bump: start-llm.ps1 load deadline 240s->480s and swap_ops.real_load_30b timeout 300s->480s, so a COLD load (289s) does not false-fail; a WARM load (12s) is unaffected.
consequence.pre_warm: the cache was pre-populated by this test, so subsequent production swaps hit the warm 12s path.
